falsificationAttempts.targetDescription: Transactional per-(run,stage) counters will preserve correctness while reducing scans
falsificationQueries: convex OCC conflicts on hot documents
convex guidance on write contention for frequently updated documents
falsificationAttempts.falsificationAnalysis: Convex documents optimistic concurrency control and explicitly calls out frequent updates to the same document as a common conflict source. A per-(run,stage) counter centralizes writes and can become a hotspot, increasing retries/permanent OCC failures under parallel completions. That weakens both throughput and correctness guarantees unless counters are sharded or otherwise contention-proofed.

falsificationAttempts.targetDescription: Counter architecture avoids scan pressure enough to be clearly superior
falsificationQueries: remaining read limits and index scan caps in Convex
whether counter reconciliation still requires broad scans
falsificationAttempts.falsificationAnalysis: Even with counters, Convex enforces strict read/scan and index-range limits per function. Reconciliation, drift repair, and migration backfills can still hit large-scan paths. This means counters do not automatically eliminate the platform-limit risk; they shift it unless migration/reconciliation is carefully bounded.
